evals.name: crud-user-api
evals.prompt: Create an HTTP controller for a User API in a Kora application. Required endpoints: GET /users/{id} (get user), POST /users (create user), DELETE /users/{id} (delete user). Use JSON request/response. User must have fields: id (String), name (String), email (String).
evals.expected_output: HTTP controller with @HttpController, @HttpRoute for 3 endpoints, @Json DTO for User request/response, @Path for path parameters

evals.name: logging-interceptor
evals.prompt: Add a global interceptor for logging all HTTP requests in a Kora application. The interceptor must log: request method, URI, execution time, response status code. Use @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) for global application.
evals.expected_output: HttpServerInterceptor implementation with logging before/after processing, @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) for global application

evals.name: error-handling
evals.prompt: Implement error handling for an HTTP controller in Kora. Required handling: 400 Bad Request for invalid data, 404 Not Found for missing resources, 500 Internal Error for unexpected errors. Use HttpServerResponseException.
evals.expected_output: Controller with throw HttpServerResponseException.of(400/404/500), optionally a global error interceptor

evals.name: query-parameters
evals.prompt: Create a GET /products endpoint for searching products with parameters: category (String, required), minPrice (BigDecimal, optional), maxPrice (BigDecimal, optional), limit (int, default 20), offset (int, default 0). Use @Query annotations.
evals.expected_output: Controller with @Query parameters, including required/optional parameters and default values

evals.name: request-headers
evals.prompt: Create a POST /api/data endpoint that accepts: a JSON body with data, header X-Request-ID (String, required), header X-User-ID (String, optional), header Accept-Language (String, default 'en'). Use @Header annotations.
evals.expected_output: Controller with @Header parameters, @Json body, handling of required/optional headers

evals.name: form-data-multipart
evals.prompt: Create a POST /upload endpoint for file uploads. The endpoint must accept: a file (multipart/form-data), file name (String), description (String, optional), tags (List<String>). Use HttpServerRequest to handle multipart.
evals.expected_output: Controller with multipart/form-data handling, parsing files and form fields

evals.name: custom-response-mapper
evals.prompt: Create a custom HttpServerResponseMapper for XML format. The mapper must serialize an object to XML and set Content-Type: application/xml. Use @ResponseCodeMapper or explicit HttpServerResponse construction.
evals.expected_output: Custom response mapper with XML serialization, correct headers

evals.name: validation-controller
evals.prompt: Create a controller with input validation. POST /users must validate: email (email format), age (18-120), name (non-empty, 2-100 characters). Return 400 with an error description when data is invalid.
evals.expected_output: Controller with field validation, 400 response with validation errors

evals.name: cors-interceptor
evals.prompt: Add a CORS interceptor for the REST API. Allow: origins https://app.example.com and https://admin.example.com, methods GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, headers Content-Type/Authorization, credentials include, maxAge 3600.
evals.expected_output: HttpServerInterceptor with CORS headers, correct Access-Control-* response headers

evals.name: async-endpoint
evals.prompt: Create an asynchronous GET /reports/{id} endpoint that performs a long-running operation in the background. Use CompletionStage to return the result. On success return 200 with JSON, on error return 500.
evals.expected_output: Controller with CompletionStage return type, async processing, error handling

evals.name: health-check-endpoint
evals.prompt: Create a simple health check endpoint GET /health that returns 200 OK with a JSON service status. Also add a readiness check GET /ready that verifies the database connection.
evals.expected_output: Simple controller with health/readiness endpoints, JSON response

evals.name: pagination-endpoint
evals.prompt: Create a GET /items endpoint with pagination: page (int, default 1), size (int, default 20, max 100), sortBy (String, optional), sortOrder (ASC/DESC, default ASC). The response must include: items array, totalPages, totalElements, currentPage.
evals.expected_output: Controller with pagination parameters, response with metadata
